verses.commentary: Pharaoh had dream. Standing by Nile.
Sleek and fat. Grazed among reeds. Healthy cattle. Nile provides life.
Seven other cows. Ugly and gaunt. Contrast.
Pharaoh woke. Impossible scene. Reversal of nature. Disturbing dream.
Fell asleep. Second dream. Healthy and good. On single stalk. Agriculture dream. Abundance.
Seven other heads. Wind from desert. Famine conditions.
Pharaoh woke. Same pattern. Consuming the good.
Morning. Mind troubled. Opening for Joseph.
Finally remembers. Two years later.
Pharaoh was once angry with his servants, and he imprisoned me and the chief baker in the house of the captain of the guard.
Each had meaning.
Young Hebrew there. Servant of captain. He interpreted. Young Hebrew.
I restored. Other man impaled. Perfect accuracy.
Rapid transition.
I had dream. Pharaoh's need.
Gives glory to God. Faith and humility.
Pharaoh recounts dream.
Fat and sleek. Grazed.
Seven other cows. Scrawny, ugly, lean.
Lean, ugly cows ate seven fat.
After eating. Woke up.
Second dream. Full and good. On single stalk.
Seven other heads. East wind damage.
None could explain.
God has revealed to Pharaoh what he is about to do. God has revealed what he is about to do. Joseph interprets. God is revealing the future.
Seven good cows are seven years. Seven good heads are seven years. Same dream. Cows and grain both represent years.
Bad interpretation. Famine coming.
God has shown what he will do. Confirmation. Divine revelation.
Throughout Egypt. Good news first. Prosperity ahead.
Abundance forgotten. Famine will ravage. Bad news.
Famine so severe. Dire warning.
God will do it soon. No preventing it.
Joseph moves from interpretation to recommendation.
Practical plan.
Store grain. In cities. Centralized storage.
Strategic planning.
Plan seemed good. Wisdom recognized.
Pagan Pharaoh recognizes divine spirit.
Since God made this known to you. Pharaoh acknowledges Joseph's God.
Only throne greater. Second in command.
I put you in charge of whole land.
Royal regalia. Official power.
Ride in chariot. Second-in-command. People shouted: Make way! Put in charge of whole land. Public ceremony.
I am Pharaoh. Without your word no one will act in Egypt. Pharaoh's supremacy maintained but Joseph's authority total.
Daughter of Potiphera, priest of On (Heliopolis). Egyptian identity.
Thirty years old. Thirteen years since sold at seventeen (37:2). Prime of life.
Prosperity arrives.
Collected all food. Stored in cities. Systematic storage. Local reserves.
Beyond measure. Echoes promise to Abraham (22:17). Unmeasurable blessing.
Before famine. Two sons born. By Asenath. Family established.
All my trouble. Or dangerous assimilation? Forgetting family.
The second son he named Ephraim and said, It is because God has made me fruitful in the land of my suffering. Blessing in exile.
Transition comes.
Egypt prepared.
Total delegation.
Famine spread. Sold grain. Famine severe. Sales, not gifts. Revenue generation.
All the world came to Egypt.
